headline: Despite the energy shock, AI arms race set to keep capex spending elevated: Strategist
summary: Matt Orton remains constructive on large-cap technology, arguing the sector is relatively insulated from geopolitical shocks. He says earnings season should clarify which firms are best placed to navigate uncertainty, even as AI-linked datacentre trades look richly valued.

headline: Forget The Chip Boom: Wall Street's 'Ultimate Contrarian Trade' Favors Software For 2026
summary: Technology sector leaders across software and semiconductors are approaching a potential inflection point, as BTIG Chief Market Technician Jonathan Krinsky outlines a contrarian shift favoring software over chip stocks. Extreme Valuation Gap Signals Reversal Setup Krinsky said the spread between software stocks in the S&P 1500 and semiconductor stocks in the SOX index has reached an unprecedented level, with the ratio falling 43% below its 200-day moving average. headline: Live near an AI data center? A new study warns one Virginia site could lead to premature deaths in the community
summary: A Vantage data center in Loudoun County — dubbed ‘Data Center Alley’ — could contribute to 33 premature deaths over five years, researchers say.

headline: Oracle Layoff Reports Put AI Governance And Investor Risks In Focus
summary: Unconfirmed reports suggest Oracle may be planning large scale layoffs that could affect up to 30,000 employees. The reported process is said to involve AI driven and algorithmic decision making in selecting which roles to cut. These reports have not yet been formally addressed by Oracle and details on timing or scope remain unclear. For investors watching NYSE:ORCL, this potential restructuring comes with the stock trading at $175.06 and showing sizeable share price moves over multiple...

headline: The Trade Driving The S&P 500 Higher May Not Last
summary: Mega-cap stocks drove recent S&P 500 outperformance, but credit markets are not confirming the equity rally. Read why risk-reward for equities may be deteriorating.

headline: Bernstein shares a 5+ year view on how AI might impact the software industry
summary: Investing.com -- The rapid ascent of generative AI has ignited an intense, ongoing debate across Wall Street regarding the long-term viability of the traditional software business model.
